title: Student Rights and Responsibilities: Student and Parent Complaints/Grievances
description: Parents and students have the right to raise concerns or complaints about decisions made by district staff, and this policy explains the steps to follow when doing so. It outlines a clear, step-by-step process for resolving issues — starting at the campus level and moving up to district leadership or the school board if needed. Having a formal process helps ensure that concerns are heard and addressed fairly and consistently.

timeline_local.summary: This policy is being updated to replace the term 'complaints' with 'grievances' throughout the document. The section header 'Complaints' and its definition paragraph are being removed. A new sentence is being added under 'Notice to Students And Parents' referencing Chapter 26 of the Texas Education Code and policy FA.
changes_added: Grievances (replacing 'Complaints' throughout all 14 numbered items and other references)
grievances (lowercase, replacing 'complaints' throughout)
This grievance policy shall be used to address alleged violations of rights guaranteed by Chapter 26 of the Texas Education Code. [See FA]
and as stated in this policy (added to informal resolution deadline exception clause)
or person standing in parental relation (added to Freedom From Retaliation section)
grievance (replacing 'complaint' throughout the document)
Grievances (replacing 'Complaints' in section heading 'Consolidating Complaints Grievances')

Human sexuality curriculum parental consent requirements: plain-language summary, identification of outside speakers, dates/class periods, statement that participation requires written parental consent under S.B. 12, 30-day advance notice requirement, right to review instructional materials, right to withhold consent without student penalty, right to revoke consent at any time, consent checkboxes/signature lines, and administrator contact information.

DEI definition per S.B. 12 (89th Texas Legislature): influencing hiring/employment with respect to race, sex, color, or ethnicity except as required by law; promoting differential treatment on basis of race, color, or ethnicity; requiring training presenting race or sex stereotyping; developing policies referencing race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation (with exceptions for HBCUs and federal/state law compliance); developing equity audits based on race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, or sexual orientation.
Prohibition on compelling any person to provide a DEI statement or giving preferential consideration based on provision of a DEI statement.
Prohibition on assigning DEI duties to any person; prohibition on district employees, contractors, or volunteers engaging in DEI duties at, for, or on behalf of the district.
Carve-out: nothing prohibits contracting with historically underutilized businesses or businesses owned by minority group members or women under applicable state law.

SEL provisions: traditional SEL consistent with district goals (self-awareness, individualism, self-reliance, self-motivation, communication, conflict resolution, interpersonal skills); prohibition on teaching divisive SEL concepts; affirmative list of approved ideologies including individualism, rejection of victimhood mentality, conflict resolution, entrepreneurship, financial self-sufficiency, importance of nuclear family, liberty/hard work/perseverance, and virtues of self-discipline/forgiveness/patience/kindness/determination/hope/thankfulness/reliability/honesty/industry/responsibility.

Definition of 'Gender Identity' as any belief/theory/ideology that: espouses gender as a social construct; espouses non-binary gender; espouses that biological sex should be changed to match a different gender; supports hormone therapy or medical procedures to alter a person's body to match a different gender.
Definition of 'sexual orientation' as sexual attraction including heterosexual, homosexual, bisexual, or other orientations.
Prohibition on Subject Persons providing instruction, guidance, activities, or programming regarding sexual orientation or gender identity to students in PreK–12.
Prohibition on Subject Persons using or permitting use of district resources to adopt, support, or promote Gender Identity or sexual orientation as defined in the policy.

Carve-outs: does not limit student First Amendment speech; does not limit authorized physical/mental health service providers subject to parental consent; does not prohibit single-sex organizations without political/social agenda from meeting on district property.
Definition of 'social transitioning' per S.B. 12: transition from biological sex at birth to opposite biological sex through different name, pronouns, or other expressions denying biological sex.
Prohibition on Subject Persons assisting students with social transitioning, including providing information about social transitioning.
Prohibition on use of pronoun identifiers inconsistent with biological sex as listed on official birth certificate or government-issued record (for both students and Subject Persons).
Prohibition on use of the term 'Latinx'; required use of 'Hispanic,' 'Hispanics,' 'Latino,' 'Latinos,' 'Latina,' or 'Latinas.'
Prohibition on use of 'minor-attracted person'; required use of 'pedophile'; instructional materials on pedophilia must refer to it only as an abhorrent crime.

Parent or employee right to report suspected social transitioning violations to the board of trustees; board shall oversee superintendent investigation; board shall report confirmed employee violations to Texas Commissioner of Education.
Referral of other concerns to complaint policies DGBA(Local), FNG(Local), and GF(Local).

Discipline provision: knowing violations are cause for discipline including termination of employees and contractors; non-employee Subject Persons may be immediately terminated or removed by superintendent or designee.
Requirement to distribute copy of this policy to all district employees, parents, volunteers, contractors, and third parties in direct contact with students.
changes_removed: In this policy, the terms 'complaint' and 'grievance' shall have the same meaning.
Complaints (in all 14 numbered items and other references, replaced by 'Grievances')
complaints (lowercase, in all instances throughout the text, replaced by 'grievances')
Complaints (removed from title 'Student And Parent Complaints/Grievances')
Complaints (removed from section heading 'Consolidating Complaints Grievances')

timeline_local.notes: This is page 1 of 6 of the FNG(Local) policy redline, page 211 of the board packet. The primary change throughout is a terminology shift from 'complaint/complaints' to 'grievance/grievances.' The definition equating the two terms is removed, implying the district is standardizing on 'grievance' as the sole term. A new sentence tying the policy to Chapter 26 TEC rights and cross-referencing policy FA is added under 'Notice to Students And Parents.'

description_local: This policy outlines the process that students and parents can use to formally raise concerns or disputes about decisions, actions, or conditions within the school district. It applies to all students and their parents or guardians who feel their rights have been affected or who disagree with how they have been treated by school staff or administrators. Understanding this policy matters because it gives families a clear, structured path to have their concerns heard and addressed fairly at the campus and district level.
